findings.message: Line 24 names the ADR front-matter convention as `knowledge/adr/adr.md`, but the same ADR later identifies the same convention/update target as `knowledge/conventions/adr.md` in Consequences/Related. This is an internal cross-document reference contradiction that makes the implementation target ambiguous; align the Context reference with the convention path used elsewhere.

D4 makes the per-decision grounding adjudication a work item of the track implementing this ADR, but the briefing defines that adjudication of the six flipped decisions as a manual follow-up that is out of scope for this track's code. As written, downstream planning can treat those unrelated ADR front-matter edits as required implementation work for this track, contradicting the intended scope. Clarify D4 so the current track only changes the precedence/value-object behavior and leaves the six-decision adjudication to the separate manual follow-up.

`OS-02` still says the single-vs-split grounding ref type decision is deferred to Phase 2, but the Phase 2 artifacts have already closed that choice on a shared `DecisionGroundRef` (`domain-types.json` also makes `AdrDecisionCommon` use `Option<DecisionGroundRef>`). The plan set is internally inconsistent about whether this architecture decision is still open.

IN-06 and AC-08 describe the fail-closed empty-placeholder behavior as belonging to `CN-04`, but `CN-04` is the domain/infrastructure placement constraint; the actual fail-closed constraint is `CN-01`. The impl plan and infrastructure catalogue already use `CN-01` for this behavior, so the spec/rendered spec currently contain a broken cross-reference that can send implementation and coverage review to the wrong constraint.

`domain-types.md` renders `DecisionGroundRef` with action `—`, but the source catalogue defines `types.DecisionGroundRef.action` as `add` and the spec/plan require adding this new value object. This makes the rendered view contradict the JSON SSoT for the central domain type in the plan artifacts.
